Grid-search (gridcv) - tecator - Plsr

using Jchemo, JchemoData
using JLD2, CairoMakie

Import data

path_jdat = dirname(dirname(pathof(JchemoData)))
db = joinpath(path_jdat, "data/tecator.jld2") 
@load db dat
@names dat

(:X, :Y)

X = dat.X
@head X

... (178, 100)

12.617762.618142.618592.619122.619812.620712.621862.623342.625112.627222.629642.632452.635652.639332.643532.648252.65352.659372.665852.672812.680082.687332.694272.700732.706842.712812.719142.726282.734622.744162.754662.765682.776792.78792.799492.812252.827062.843562.861062.878572.894972.909242.920852.930152.938462.947712.960192.978313.003063.035063.074283.119633.168683.217713.262543.299883.328473.348993.363423.373793.381523.387413.391643.394183.39493.393663.390453.385413.378693.370413.360733.349793.337693.324433.310133.294873.278913.262323.245423.228283.21083.192873.174333.155033.134753.113393.091163.06853.045963.023933.002472.981452.960722.940132.919782.899662.879642.85962.83942.8192
22.834542.838712.842832.847052.851382.855872.86062.865662.870932.876612.882642.888982.895772.903082.910972.919532.928732.938632.949292.960722.972722.984932.99693.008333.01923.02993.041013.053453.067773.084163.102213.121063.139833.15813.176233.195193.215843.237473.258893.278353.293843.303623.306813.303933.2973.289253.284093.285053.293263.309233.332673.362513.396613.431883.464923.492953.514583.530043.540673.547973.553063.556753.559213.560453.560343.558763.555713.551323.545853.53953.532353.524423.515833.506683.4973.486833.476263.465523.455013.444813.434773.424653.414193.403033.390823.377313.362653.347453.332453.318183.304733.291863.279213.266553.253693.240453.226593.211813.1963.17942
32.582842.584582.586292.588082.589962.591922.594012.596272.598732.601312.604142.607142.610292.613612.617142.620892.624862.629092.633612.638352.64332.648382.653542.65872.663752.66882.673832.678922.684112.689372.69472.700122.705632.711412.717752.72492.733442.743272.754332.766422.779312.792722.806492.820642.835412.851212.868722.889052.912892.940882.973253.009463.04783.085543.119473.146963.166773.179383.186313.189243.18953.188013.184983.180393.174113.166113.156413.145123.132413.118433.103293.087143.070143.052373.033933.015042.995692.976122.956422.93662.916672.896552.876222.855632.834742.813612.792352.771132.750152.729562.709342.689512.670092.651122.632622.614612.597182.580342.564042.54816
Y = dat.Y 
@head Y

... (178, 4)
3×4 DataFrame
Rowwaterfatproteintyp
Float64Float64Float64String
160.522.516.7train
246.040.113.5train
371.08.420.5train
typ = Y.typ
tab(typ)

OrderedCollections.OrderedDict{String, Int64} with 3 entries:
  "test"  => 31
  "train" => 115
  "val"   => 32

wlst = names(X)
wl = parse.(Float64, wlst) ;
#plotsp(X, wl; xlabel = "Wavelength (nm)", ylabel = "Absorbance").f

Preprocessing

model1 = snv()
model2 = savgol(npoint = 15, deriv = 2, degree = 3)
model = pip(model1, model2)
fit!(model, X)
@head Xp = transf(model, X) 
#plotsp(Xp, wl; xlabel = "Wavelength (nm)", ylabel = "Absorbance").f

3×100 Matrix{Float64}:
 0.000397076  0.000495203  0.000598623  …  0.00827138  0.00917311  0.00946072
 0.00242055   0.00244366   0.00234233      0.00580631  0.00689249  0.00749316
 0.0011927    0.00122721   0.00120098      0.0101019   0.0108142   0.0108444
... (178, 100)

Split Tot to Train/Test

The model is fitted on Train, and the generalization error is estimated on Test. In this example, Train is already defined in variable typ of the dataset, and Test is defined by the remaining samples. But Tot could also be split a posteriori, for instance by sampling (random, systematic or any other designs). See for instance functions samprand, sampsys, etc.

s = typ .== "train"
Xtrain = Xp[s, :] 
Ytrain = Y[s, :]
Xtest = rmrow(Xp, s)
Ytest = rmrow(Y, s)
ntrain = nro(Xtrain)
ntest = nro(Xtest)
ntot = ntrain + ntest
(ntot = ntot, ntrain, ntest)

(ntot = 178, ntrain = 115, ntest = 63)

Working response y

namy = names(Y)[1:3]
j = 2  
nam = namy[j]    # work on the j-th y-variable
ytrain = Ytrain[:, nam]
ytest = Ytest[:, nam] ;

CV-Segments for model tuning

Two methods can be used to build the CV segments within Train (for the same number of segments, these two methods return close resuts):

  • (1) Replicated K-fold CV

    • Train is splitted in a number of K folds (segments).

    • This split can be replicated (==> replicated K-Fold CV).

K = 3     # nb. folds (segments)
rep = 25  # nb. replications
segm = segmkf(ntrain, K; rep = rep)

25-element Vector{Vector{Vector{Int64}}}:
 [[5, 9, 11, 12, 14, 20, 21, 23, 26, 27  …  86, 87, 92, 94, 95, 97, 104, 106, 107, 110], [2, 3, 7, 13, 15, 16, 18, 22, 25, 28  …  84, 85, 88, 89, 93, 100, 101, 103, 111, 112], [1, 4, 6, 8, 10, 17, 19, 24, 32, 37  …  96, 98, 99, 102, 105, 108, 109, 113, 114, 115]]
 [[1, 3, 5, 6, 14, 18, 20, 21, 25, 26  …  87, 89, 90, 92, 94, 96, 99, 104, 106, 108], [2, 4, 7, 10, 13, 15, 17, 22, 23, 24  …  71, 77, 78, 79, 91, 97, 102, 103, 107, 113], [8, 9, 11, 12, 16, 19, 28, 36, 39, 44  …  98, 100, 101, 105, 109, 110, 111, 112, 114, 115]]
 [[1, 2, 4, 6, 12, 15, 17, 18, 19, 20  …  83, 96, 97, 101, 103, 104, 107, 108, 109, 112], [3, 7, 8, 16, 22, 24, 27, 28, 29, 31  …  92, 93, 100, 102, 105, 110, 111, 113, 114, 115], [5, 9, 10, 11, 13, 14, 21, 26, 30, 34  …  85, 86, 87, 88, 89, 94, 95, 98, 99, 106]]
 [[4, 6, 10, 13, 15, 16, 17, 21, 23, 24  …  87, 88, 91, 92, 93, 95, 97, 102, 113, 115], [3, 7, 9, 14, 18, 25, 30, 35, 38, 41  …  103, 104, 105, 106, 107, 108, 109, 110, 111, 112], [1, 2, 5, 8, 11, 12, 19, 20, 22, 28  …  75, 77, 78, 79, 80, 81, 84, 86, 90, 114]]
 [[1, 4, 8, 10, 16, 17, 19, 20, 21, 23  …  68, 76, 78, 82, 89, 92, 94, 97, 98, 100], [2, 11, 32, 35, 37, 42, 46, 48, 50, 52  …  88, 90, 102, 104, 105, 107, 109, 113, 114, 115], [3, 5, 6, 7, 9, 12, 13, 14, 15, 18  …  95, 96, 99, 101, 103, 106, 108, 110, 111, 112]]
 [[2, 5, 8, 13, 16, 24, 25, 26, 27, 33  …  81, 87, 91, 93, 94, 98, 105, 107, 111, 113], [1, 6, 7, 11, 12, 14, 15, 20, 23, 32  …  88, 95, 96, 97, 99, 100, 101, 103, 106, 108], [3, 4, 9, 10, 17, 18, 19, 21, 22, 28  …  89, 90, 92, 102, 104, 109, 110, 112, 114, 115]]
 [[5, 6, 9, 13, 14, 20, 25, 26, 30, 32  …  89, 96, 100, 102, 103, 104, 106, 109, 111, 115], [1, 3, 7, 10, 11, 12, 19, 21, 23, 27  …  81, 84, 85, 87, 88, 91, 98, 107, 110, 113], [2, 4, 8, 15, 16, 17, 18, 22, 24, 28  …  93, 94, 95, 97, 99, 101, 105, 108, 112, 114]]
 [[3, 8, 10, 13, 15, 20, 23, 27, 28, 32  …  82, 87, 88, 89, 95, 97, 104, 110, 111, 113], [5, 11, 16, 24, 25, 26, 29, 31, 36, 37  …  94, 96, 98, 99, 100, 102, 103, 109, 114, 115], [1, 2, 4, 6, 7, 9, 12, 14, 17, 18  …  81, 84, 90, 91, 101, 105, 106, 107, 108, 112]]
 [[1, 5, 6, 14, 15, 17, 18, 19, 22, 28  …  84, 87, 89, 92, 93, 98, 101, 104, 112, 115], [3, 4, 7, 9, 12, 20, 31, 32, 33, 38  …  94, 95, 96, 97, 100, 103, 106, 110, 111, 113], [2, 8, 10, 11, 13, 16, 21, 23, 24, 25  …  78, 79, 90, 99, 102, 105, 107, 108, 109, 114]]
 [[2, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 18, 20, 22  …  94, 95, 100, 101, 102, 103, 104, 107, 112, 113], [3, 4, 7, 16, 23, 25, 28, 31, 35, 36  …  84, 87, 89, 90, 91, 97, 105, 106, 110, 114], [1, 5, 6, 14, 15, 17, 19, 21, 27, 29  …  77, 85, 88, 96, 98, 99, 108, 109, 111, 115]]
 ⋮
 [[5, 6, 7, 8, 11, 16, 22, 26, 30, 31  …  92, 98, 102, 103, 105, 107, 109, 110, 112, 114], [1, 2, 9, 13, 15, 17, 23, 28, 32, 33  …  84, 93, 94, 96, 100, 101, 106, 111, 113, 115], [3, 4, 10, 12, 14, 18, 19, 20, 21, 24  …  79, 80, 83, 85, 88, 95, 97, 99, 104, 108]]
 [[2, 3, 5, 8, 11, 12, 16, 19, 25, 26  …  86, 88, 96, 97, 99, 101, 108, 112, 113, 115], [1, 4, 6, 14, 21, 22, 28, 32, 39, 40  …  92, 93, 94, 100, 102, 104, 107, 109, 111, 114], [7, 9, 10, 13, 15, 17, 18, 20, 23, 24  …  85, 87, 89, 91, 95, 98, 103, 105, 106, 110]]
 [[6, 7, 9, 10, 13, 14, 16, 17, 22, 24  …  83, 85, 88, 90, 91, 99, 100, 101, 106, 115], [2, 4, 5, 12, 15, 20, 21, 25, 26, 27  …  95, 96, 97, 98, 103, 108, 109, 110, 112, 113], [1, 3, 8, 11, 18, 19, 23, 29, 32, 35  …  84, 89, 92, 93, 102, 104, 105, 107, 111, 114]]
 [[1, 3, 4, 8, 9, 10, 13, 16, 24, 25  …  81, 84, 86, 88, 90, 93, 94, 102, 110, 113], [5, 6, 11, 14, 19, 20, 23, 26, 27, 34  …  89, 91, 95, 96, 99, 104, 105, 106, 109, 111], [2, 7, 12, 15, 17, 18, 21, 22, 28, 29  …  97, 98, 100, 101, 103, 107, 108, 112, 114, 115]]
 [[2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 8, 12, 20, 21, 22  …  79, 83, 86, 87, 88, 96, 97, 102, 114, 115], [6, 11, 16, 17, 24, 25, 26, 28, 32, 38  …  90, 91, 93, 95, 98, 100, 106, 108, 110, 111], [1, 9, 10, 13, 14, 15, 18, 19, 29, 33  …  94, 99, 101, 103, 104, 105, 107, 109, 112, 113]]
 [[1, 6, 17, 19, 26, 27, 31, 32, 34, 38  …  93, 94, 96, 99, 102, 105, 106, 107, 108, 112], [2, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 16, 18, 22, 23  …  84, 87, 98, 100, 104, 109, 110, 113, 114, 115], [4, 8, 10, 12, 13, 14, 15, 20, 21, 25  …  81, 83, 88, 90, 92, 95, 97, 101, 103, 111]]
 [[10, 14, 15, 17, 18, 20, 23, 34, 37, 38  …  87, 91, 92, 99, 103, 104, 109, 110, 111, 115], [2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 8, 9, 12, 19, 22  …  77, 80, 85, 89, 90, 94, 96, 98, 102, 112], [1, 6, 11, 13, 16, 21, 25, 27, 28, 29  …  95, 97, 100, 101, 105, 106, 107, 108, 113, 114]]
 [[11, 15, 16, 21, 22, 24, 25, 26, 33, 35  …  88, 90, 93, 97, 100, 102, 103, 107, 109, 111], [5, 7, 8, 12, 13, 17, 19, 27, 29, 30  …  79, 85, 94, 95, 104, 106, 108, 110, 113, 115], [1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 9, 10, 14, 18, 20  …  89, 91, 92, 96, 98, 99, 101, 105, 112, 114]]
 [[1, 8, 13, 14, 15, 18, 19, 20, 21, 23  …  81, 84, 92, 97, 99, 100, 101, 103, 107, 110], [2, 5, 10, 11, 12, 16, 17, 22, 25, 34  …  91, 94, 98, 102, 105, 106, 108, 109, 112, 114], [3, 4, 6, 7, 9, 24, 26, 29, 31, 35  …  86, 87, 90, 93, 95, 96, 104, 111, 113, 115]]

length(segm)

25

  • (2) Replicated "Test-set" CV

    • Train is split to Cal/Val (e.g. Cal = 70% of Train, Val = 30% of Train).

    • This split can be replicated (==> replicated test-set CV). 

#pct = .30
#m = Int(round(pct * ntrain))
#segm = segmts(ntrain, m; rep = 30)

Illustration of segments:

## The K segments of replication 'i'
i = 1  
segm[i]

3-element Vector{Vector{Int64}}:
 [5, 9, 11, 12, 14, 20, 21, 23, 26, 27  …  86, 87, 92, 94, 95, 97, 104, 106, 107, 110]
 [2, 3, 7, 13, 15, 16, 18, 22, 25, 28  …  84, 85, 88, 89, 93, 100, 101, 103, 111, 112]
 [1, 4, 6, 8, 10, 17, 19, 24, 32, 37  …  96, 98, 99, 102, 105, 108, 109, 113, 114, 115]

## Segment 'k' (k = 1, ..., K) of replication 'i''
k = 1
segm[i][k]

39-element Vector{Int64}:
   5
   9
  11
  12
  14
  20
  21
  23
  26
  27
   ⋮
  87
  92
  94
  95
  97
 104
 106
 107
 110

Grid-search

The generic syntax to use function gridcv is to set all parameters of the grid in argument pars built with function mpar. But for LV-based functions (eg. plskern, kplsr, lwplsr, etc.) it is more efficient to set parameter nlv outside argument pars. This optimizes the computation time.

The same principle applies when defining parameter lb in ridge-based functions (rr, krr, etc.). To set parameter lb outside argument pars optimizes the computation time.

nlv = 0:20
model = plskern()
rescv = gridcv(model, Xtrain, ytrain; segm, score = rmsep, nlv)
@names rescv 

@head res = rescv.res            # Averages over the replicatins and segments

@head res_rep = rescv.res_rep  # Results for each replication and segment (useful 
                               # to visualize the variability of the results across segments 
                               # and replications)

... (21, 2)
 
... (1575, 4)
3×2 DataFrame
Rownlvy1
Int64Float64
1012.6898
212.53742
322.38063
3×4 DataFrame
Rowrepsegmnlvy1
Int64Int64Int64Float64
111012.767
21113.03976
31122.61319
plotgrid(res.nlv, res.y1; step = 2, xlabel = "Nb. LVs", ylabel = "RMSEP-CV").f

f, ax = plotgrid(res.nlv, res.y1; step = 2, xlabel = "Nb. LVs", ylabel = "RMSEP-CV")
for i = 1:rep, j = 1:K
    zres = res_rep[res_rep.rep .== i .&& res_rep.segm .== j, :]
    lines!(ax, zres.nlv, zres.y1; color = (:grey, .2))
end
lines!(ax, res.nlv, res.y1; color = :red, linewidth = 1)
f

Naïve syntax (not efficient for computation time)

To set nlv in argument pars is strictly equivalent (gives the same results) but is less efficient for computation time. 

pars = mpar(nlv = 0:20)
model = plskern()
@head gridcv(model, Xtrain, ytrain; segm, score = rmsep, pars).res

... (21, 2)
3×2 DataFrame
Rownlvy1
Int64Float64
1012.6898
212.53742
322.38063

Final prediction (Test) using the optimal model

Select the best parameter combination

u = findall(res.y1 .== minimum(res.y1))[1] 
res[u, :]
DataFrameRow (2 columns)
Rownlvy1
Int64Float64
1092.20683

Fit the optimal model on the whole Train set

model = plskern(nlv = res.nlv[u])
fit!(model, Xtrain, ytrain)
@head pred = predict(model, Xtest).pred

3×1 Matrix{Float64}:
 28.035152559327607
  2.5700025992964033
  4.38080973413507
... (63, 1)

Generalization error (Test set)

rmsep(pred, ytest)

1×1 Matrix{Float64}:
 1.8712902096014705

Plotting predictions vs. observed data

plotxy(pred, ytest; size = (500, 400), color = (:red, .5), bisect = true, title = "Test set - variable $nam", 
    xlabel = "Prediction", ylabel = "Observed").f

Additional parameters in the grid

If other parameters have to be defined in the grid, they must be set in argument pars built with function mpar, such as in the example below.

pars = mpar(scal = [false; true])
nlv = 0:20
model = plskern()
@head res = gridcv(model, Xtrain, ytrain; segm, score = rmsep, pars, nlv).res

... (42, 3)
3×3 DataFrame
Rownlvscaly1
Int64BoolFloat64
10false12.6898
20true12.6898
31false2.53742
plotgrid(res.nlv, res.y1, res.scal; step = 2, xlabel = "Nb. LVs", ylabel = "RMSEP-CV").f

u = findall(res.y1 .== minimum(res.y1))[1] 
res[u, :]
DataFrameRow (3 columns)
Rownlvscaly1
Int64BoolFloat64
199false2.20683
model = plskern(nlv = res.nlv[u], scal = res.scal[u])
fit!(model, Xtrain, ytrain)
@head pred = predict(model, Xtest).pred

3×1 Matrix{Float64}:
 28.035152559327607
  2.5700025992964033
  4.38080973413507
... (63, 1)

Published from gridcv_tecator_plsr.jmd using Weave.jl v0.10.12 on 2026-04-28.
